status: TIER 1 PRIMARY-SOURCE BATCH A COMPLETE WITHIN DEFINED SCOPE / SUPPLIER PRODUCT PASSPORT OPEN / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
evidence_maturity: Defined U.S. approved context and substantial human evidence; pivotal primary extraction remains partial in Build 0018.
key_translation_boundary: Population-specific clinical evidence must not be broadened to ordinary obesity or cosmetic fat loss.
source_closure_scope: pivotal or major randomised human evidence
current major safety and contraindication sources
authoritative_product_boundary: EGRIFTA WR is an exact tesamorelin prescription biologic for reduction of excess abdominal fat in adults with HIV-associated lipodystrophy. The current label states that it is not indicated for weight-loss management and that the 11.6 mg EGRIFTA WR formulation has product-specific instructions. Supplier TSM powders do not inherit the authorised formulation, indication, excipients, quality or label.
human_evidence_boundary: Two randomised placebo-controlled programmes in people with HIV and excess abdominal fat demonstrated reductions in visceral adipose tissue. The pivotal evidence is tied to protocol-defined tesamorelin material, populations, duration and clinical monitoring.
safety_source_boundary: Current controls include active malignancy contraindication, IGF-1 elevation, glucose intolerance/diabetes risk, hypersensitivity, fluid retention and injection-site reactions. Long-term cardiovascular safety has not been established.
